channel.title: World - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.description:

  2. Arctic ocean passes 'irreversible' chemical tipping point  Oceanographic Magazine
  3. Sea ice loss drives a regime shift in Arctic Ocean nitrogen biogeochemistry | Communications Earth & Environment  Nature
  4. Arctic Ocean passed a tipping point and scientists say it may never recover  ScienceDaily
  5. Melting Arctic sea ice is stripping a key nutrient from the ocean  Earth.com

item.title: Iceland's parliament votes to hold referendum on EU accession talks in August - Reuters
item.description:
1. Iceland's parliament votes to hold referendum on EU accession talks in August  Reuters
  2. A Cod Boat, a Coast Guard Flight and a Lot of Debate About the E.U.  The New York Times
  3. Iceland’s foreign minister fears ‘Brexit moment’ in EU accession referendum  The Guardian
  4. Trump Has Accidentally Strengthened the EU  The New Republic
  5. Nation Forced to Act After Trump, 79, Confuses Them for Target  The Daily Beast

item.title: This isn't about a few 'bad apples.' Israel is annexing the West Bank - Responsible Statecraft
item.description:
1. This isn't about a few 'bad apples.' Israel is annexing the West Bank  Responsible Statecraft
  2. In the West Bank, Israeli settlers speed up land grab from Palestinians  NPR
  3. Lior Amihai, director of Israeli NGO Peace Now: 'The coalition in power wants to annex the territories in the West Bank and establish a racist state based on Jewish supremacy'  Le Monde.fr
  4. The Israeli Far-Right’s Systematic Effort to Annex the West Bank  Substack
  5. West Bank Land Loss Deepens as Israel Secretly Approves Record Settlements  Clarion India

item.title: U.S. Tourist Arrested for Giving Diet Coke to Tribe Breaks Silence - The Daily Beast
item.description:
1. U.S. Tourist Arrested for Giving Diet Coke to Tribe Breaks Silence  The Daily Beast
  2. YouTuber who gave can of Coke to secluded tribe reveals real reason he did it  Daily Express
  3. YouTuber defends giving Coke to uncontacted tribe which could have wiped them out  Daily Star
  4. Moment YouTuber lands on forbidden island to leave coke can for isolated tribe  the-sun.com
  5. Man traveling to the “world’s most remote island” was arrested  latination.com

item.description:

item.title: IDF has solution for Hezbollah's FPV drones, Eyal Zamir says from Lebanon - The Jerusalem Post
item.description:
1. IDF has solution for Hezbollah's FPV drones, Eyal Zamir says from Lebanon  The Jerusalem Post

  4. Soldier killed in Hezbollah drone attack as Israel widens strikes on terror group  The Times of Israel
  5. IDF Sergeant Rotem Yanai, 20, was killed after she fell during an operational activity in northern Israel  i24NEWS

item.title: Ukraine Hits Russian Refineries, Oil Depots and Port in Massive 208-Drone Overnight Assault - Kyiv Post
item.description:
1. Ukraine Hits Russian Refineries, Oil Depots and Port in Massive 208-Drone Overnight Assault  Kyiv Post
  2. One killed, two injured in Ukrainian drone attack on Russia's Volgograd region, governor says  Reuters
  3. Ukrainian drone attack triggers fire at a Russian oil terminal  AP News
  4. A new Meduza analysis finds Ukraine’s long-range strikes are reaching twice as deep but not surging in 2026. Russia’s refineries, meanwhile, keep bouncing back.  Meduza
  5. Strategic Blow to Russian Energy Sector After New Ukrainian Strike on Tuapse Refinery  UNITED24 Media

item.title: Russian rate of losses in Ukraine almost triples in one year - Al Jazeera
item.description:
1. Russian rate of losses in Ukraine almost triples in one year  Al Jazeera
  2. Nearly 500,000 Russian soldiers have died in Ukraine war, British intelligence agency says  CBS News
  3. Nearly 500,000 Russian soldiers killed in Ukraine, top UK intel chief says  Defense News
  4. Nearly half a million Russians killed in Ukraine war, UK spy chief says  The Guardian
  5. Almost half million Russian soldiers killed in Ukraine war, UK spy chief says  The Hill
